decks.name: Level 1 — Beginner Words
decks.description: Simple CVC words, consonant teams, and basic vowel teams

cards.back:
C + A + T
/k/ + /a/ + /t/
C says /k/ before A (Rule 1)

B + E + D
/b/ + /e/ + /d/
Closed syllable — E says short /e/
cards.hint: Short vowel in a closed syllable

cards.back:
S + I + T
/s/ + /i/ + /t/
Closed syllable — I says short /i/

H + O + T
/h/ + /o/ + /t/
Closed syllable — O says short /o/
cards.hint: Short O in a closed syllable

cards.back:
C + U + P
/k/ + /u/ + /p/
C says /k/ before U

J + A + M
/j/ + /a/ + /m/
J always says /j/

V + A + N
/v/ + /a/ + /n/
V says /v/ — simple consonant

H + U + G
/h/ + /u/ + /g/
G says /g/ at end of word

Y + A + M
/y/ + /a/ + /m/
Y as consonant at start of word
cards.hint: Y is a consonant when it starts a word

cards.back:
K + I + T
/k/ + /i/ + /t/
Closed syllable keeps I short
cards.hint: K says /k/ — same sound as C before A, O, U

cards.back:
P + E + N
/p/ + /e/ + /n/
Closed syllable — short E

L + O + G
/l/ + /o/ + /g/
Closed syllable — short O

R + U + N
/r/ + /u/ + /n/
Closed syllable — short U

SH + I + P
/sh/ + /i/ + /p/
SH consonant team says /sh/
cards.hint: SH is one phonogram making one sound

cards.back:
TH + A + T
/th/ + /a/ + /t/
TH says voiced /th/ here

CH + O + P
/ch/ + /o/ + /p/
CH consonant team says /ch/

R + I + NG
/r/ + /i/ + /ng/
NG consonant team says /ng/
cards.hint: NG is a nasal sound at the back of throat

cards.back:
D + U + CK
/d/ + /u/ + /k/
CK after single short vowel (Rule 6)
cards.hint: CK is the bodyguard for short vowels

cards.back:
R + AI + N
/r/ + /ay/ + /n/
AI used in middle of word (Rule 9)
cards.hint: AI in the middle, AY at the end

cards.back:
P + L + AY
/p/ + /l/ + /ay/
AY at end of base word (Rule 9)
cards.hint: AY at the end of a base word

cards.back:
T + R + EE
/t/ + /r/ + /ee/
EE vowel team — always says /ee/
cards.hint: EE is one of the most reliable vowel teams

cards.back:
G + OA + T
/g/ + /oh/ + /t/
OA vowel team says long O
cards.hint: OA — first vowel says its name

cards.back:
C + OI + N
/k/ + /oy/ + /n/
OI in middle of word (Rule 10)
cards.hint: OI in the middle, OY at the end

cards.back:
B + OY
/b/ + /oy/
OY at end of word (Rule 10)
cards.hint: OY at the end — same sound as OI

cards.back:
C + OW
/k/ + /ow/
OW can say /ow/ or /oh/
cards.hint: Here OW says /ow/ like in OWL

cards.back:
C + AR
/k/ + /ar/
AR — R controls the vowel sound
cards.hint: R-controlled vowel — R changes A's sound

cards.back:
H + ER
/h/ + /er/
ER — one of the /er/ triplets
cards.hint: ER, IR, UR all sound the same

cards.back:
B + IR + D
/b/ + /er/ + /d/
IR — sounds same as ER and UR

B + UR + N
/b/ + /er/ + /n/
UR — third /er/ triplet

F + OR + K
/f/ + /or/ + /k/
OR — R controls the O
cards.hint: R changes O to the /or/ sound

cards.back:
F + L + Y
/f/ + /l/ + /eye/
Y says /eye/ in one-syllable words
cards.hint: Y acts as a vowel saying /eye/

cards.back:
QU + I + T
/kw/ + /i/ + /t/
QU always together — says /kw/

F + OO + D
/f/ + /oo/ + /d/
OO says /oo/ as in moon

B + OO + K
/b/ + /oo/ + /k/
OO says short /oo/ as in book

OU + T
/ow/ + /t/
OU vowel team says /ow/
cards.hint: OU in the middle of words

cards.back:
S + AW
/s/ + /aw/
AW says /aw/ — end position partner of AU
cards.hint: AW at the end, AU in the middle

cards.back:
N + EW
/n/ + /oo/
EW vowel team says /oo/
cards.hint: EW can also say /yoo/ in some words

cards.back:
K + I + N + D
/k/ + /eye/ + /n/ + /d/
I says long /eye/ before ND
cards.hint: I before ND says its name

cards.back:
C + O + L + D
/k/ + /oh/ + /l/ + /d/
O says long /oh/ before LD
cards.hint: O before LD says its name

cards.back:
GN + A + T
/n/ + /a/ + /t/
GN consonant team — G is silent

decks.name: Level 2 — Silent E & Shields
decks.description: Silent E words, shield patterns (CK, DGE, TCH), and suffix basics

cards.back:
SH + A + K + E
Silent E makes A say its name /ay/ (Rule 17)

PH + O + N + E
PH says /f/; silent E makes O long /oh/

WR + I + T + E
Silent W in WR + silent E for long I
cards.hint: Two silent letters in one word!

cards.back:
KN + I + F + E
Silent K in KN + silent E for long I

G + R + A + P + E
Silent E makes A long /ay/

F + L + U + T + E
Silent E makes U say /oo/

G + L + O + V + E
E guards V — English words don't end in V (Rule 3/18)
cards.hint: Silent E Job 2 — V guard, NOT long O

cards.back:
D + A + N + C + E
E keeps C soft /s/ at end (Rule 19)
cards.hint: Silent E Job 3 — keeps C soft

cards.back:
S + T + A + G + E
E softens G to /j/ and makes A long
cards.hint: E does TWO jobs — long A and soft G

cards.back:
B + A + DGE
DGE after short vowel for /j/ (Rule 8)

F + U + DGE
DGE protects short U

M + A + TCH
TCH after short A (Rule 7)

C + A + TCH
TCH after short A for /ch/

H + O + P + ED
hope → hoped: drop E, add -ED (Rule 12)
cards.hint: Silent E dropped before vowel suffix

cards.back:
B + I + G + G + ER
big + er: double G before vowel suffix (Rule 14)

R + U + N + N + I + NG
run + ing: double N before vowel suffix (Rule 14)

W + A + N + T + ED
ED says /ed/ after T — adds a syllable (Rule 22)
cards.hint: Base ends in T, so ED = /ed/

cards.back:
P + L + AY + ED
ED says /d/ after voiced sound

J + U + M + P + ED
ED says /t/ after unvoiced P

F + O + X + E + S
Add -ES after X — hissing sound (Rule 23)
cards.hint: X already hisses, so add -ES for a new syllable

cards.back:
N + IGH + T
IGH — three letters, one sound /eye/ (Rule 37)

L + IGH + T
IGH with GH silent
cards.hint: One of the most reliable patterns

cards.back:
SH + OU + T
OU says /ow/ in the middle

T + R + AI + N
AI in middle of word (Rule 9)

C + R + EA + M
EA vowel team — long E /ee/

B + L + UE
UE vowel team — E guards U (Rule 3/18)
cards.hint: English words don't end in U alone

cards.back:
F + R + UI + T
UI vowel team says /oo/
cards.hint: UI is a less common vowel team

cards.back:
GH + O + S + T
GH says /g/ at start; O long before ST

C + L + I + FF
FF after short vowel, one syllable (Rule 5)

S + P + E + LL
LL after short E in one-syllable word (Rule 5)

decks.name: Level 3 — Y Changes & Combos
decks.description: Y-to-I changes, -FUL suffix, GH combos, and multi-rule words

cards.back:
H + A + PP + I + ER
happy → happier: Y changes to I (Rule 15)

C + AR + R + IE + D
carry → carried: Y → I before -ED (Rule 15)
cards.hint: Y becomes I before any suffix except -ING

cards.back:
S + T + U + D + IE + D
study → studied: Y → I + ED suffix (Rule 15)

H + A + PP + I + L + Y
happy → happily: Y → I before -LY (Rule 15)
cards.hint: Y changes to I before -LY too

cards.back:
S + T + AY + I + NG
stay + ing: AY keeps Y (Rule 16)

C + AR + E + F + U + L
FULL → -FUL as suffix, drops one L (Rule 27)

H + O + P + E + F + U + L
-FUL suffix; keep silent E before consonant suffix (Rule 13)

TH + OUGH + T
OUGH says /aw/ here
cards.hint: OUGH has six possible sounds — /aw/ is one

cards.back:
TH + R + OUGH
OUGH says /oo/ here

D + AUGH + T + ER
AUGH says /aw/ (Rule 37)

C + AUGH + T
AUGH — four letters for /aw/ sound

F + R + EIGH + T
EIGH says /ay/
cards.hint: Four letters for /ay/ — GH is silent

cards.back:
KN + IGH + T
K silent in KN + IGH says /eye/
cards.hint: Silent K plus silent GH — tricky word!

cards.back:
A + L + R + EA + D + Y
ALL → AL as prefix, drops one L

N + A + TI + O + N
TI says /sh/ at start of suffix

S + P + E + CI + A + L
CI says /sh/ at start of suffix

V + I + SI + O + N
SI says /zh/ — voiced version of /sh/

W + A + TCH + E + S
-ES after TCH (hissing sound) (Rule 23)

QU + I + CK + L + Y
QU team + CK after short I + Y says /ee/
cards.hint: Multiple phonogram teams in one word

cards.back:
M + I + D + N + IGH + T
Compound word with IGH

decks.name: Level 4 — Advanced Patterns
decks.description: Schwa sounds, borrowed words, and complex multi-rule words

cards.back:
B + A + LL + OO + N
First A is schwa in unstressed syllable

C + A + P + T + AI + N
Second A is schwa — sounds like /uh/
cards.hint: AI in unstressed position loses its usual sound

cards.back:
E + L + E + PH + A + N + T
PH says /f/; second E is schwa

C + O + N + T + I + N + UE
UE guards U — English words don't end in U (Rule 3)
cards.hint: UE at the end protects U

cards.back:
B + UI + L + D + I + NG
UI vowel team says /i/ here
cards.hint: UI unusually says short /i/ in BUILD

cards.back:
WR + I + NK + L + E
W silent in WR; NK says /ngk/

A + L + TH + OUGH
AL- prefix + OUGH says /oh/
cards.hint: OUGH here says /oh/ — yet another OUGH sound

cards.back:
R + E + S + C + UE
UE — E guards U at end of word
cards.hint: U can't end a word alone

cards.back:
J + UI + C + E
UI vowel team says /oo/

D + O + NK + EY
NK says /ngk/; EY says /ee/

decks.name: Level 5 — Expert Words
decks.description: Complex words combining multiple rules and patterns

cards.back:
PH + I + L + O + S + O + PH + Y
Greek PH = /f/ (appears twice!)

CH + A + R + A + C + T + ER
Greek CH = /k/ (not /ch/)

C + O + M + F + OR + T + A + B + LE
-ABLE joins complete word COMFORT (Rule 26)

B + EA + U + T + I + F + U + L
beauty → beautiful: Y → I before -FUL (Rule 15)

A + CC + I + D + E + N + T + AL + LY
L + LY = LLY (Rule 28)

N + O + T + I + CE + A + B + LE
E kept to preserve soft C before -ABLE
cards.hint: Exception to Rule 12 — E stays to keep C soft

cards.back:
C + OU + R + A + GE + OU + S
E kept to preserve soft G
cards.hint: Exception to Rule 12 — E stays to keep G soft

cards.back:
B + E + G + I + N + N + I + NG
Stress on last syllable → double N (Rule 14)
cards.hint: begin + ning: stressed ending, so double

cards.back:
KN + OW + L + E + DGE
Silent K in KN; OW = /o/; DGE after short vowel
cards.hint: Multiple rules: silent KN + DGE shield

cards.back:
CH + A + M + P + I + O + N + SH + I + P
CH team + SH team in one word
cards.hint: Two different consonant teams working together
